construct: Demonstrates proficiency in designing, developing, and applying computational solutions to real-world problems, guided by Computer Science principles and prioritising ethics and responsible digital citizenship.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Recognises computer system components to build and troubleshoot computer systems everyday life

subSkills.label: Identifies components, understands functions, assembles and troubleshoots systems

elementsOfConstruct.description: Analyses, computer tools based on their roles in problem solving

subSkills.label: Analyses, classifies and distinguishes computer tools by problem-solving role

elementsOfConstruct.description: Analyses computing concepts by identifying relationships, principles and underlying structures

subSkills.label: Investigates core computing concepts and how they interconnect in digital systems

elementsOfConstruct.description: Applies computational knowledge to critically evaluate situations and make informed technological decisions

subSkills.label: Computational thinking to evaluate options on efficiency, ethics, safety and impact

elementsOfConstruct.description: Creates and designs systems that solve real life problems and improve way of life for citizens

subSkills.label: Designs/develops creative functional user-centred digital solutions for societal needs

components.structureNotes: SBA Form 2: 9 tasks; Form 3: 7 tasks (incl. project). Final Exam 5 items/100, 2h30. Weighting 30:70.

exemplars.scenarioTheme: Design a digital solution for a Zambian community problem (user-centred)
